summary: Crisp, crystal‑clear soundboard captures a tight, high‑energy show highlighted by Pigpen’s vocals and standout versions of Good Lovin’, Bertha and El Paso.
review: The Miller transfer delivers an excellent soundboard with remarkable clarity—especially the drum mix—and showcases a band that’s loose, enthusiastic and full of Pigpen‑centric joy. While the set lacks the long second‑set jams Deadheads often crave, the tight playing and strong song choices (Good Lovin’, Bertha, China Cat > I Know You Rider, El Paso) make it a very enjoyable listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of the 12 reviews award 4‑5 stars and praise both the superb sound quality and the energetic performance, outweighing a few comments about the lack of jams.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean board mix with exceptional drum presence (Kreutzmann highlighted). Vocals are clear though Jerry’s mic can be a bit over‑driven on Bertha. Minor PA burps noted in one review but overall superb fidelity.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Tight, upbeat first set that builds into a lively second set; the show feels like a warm‑up for Europe—high energy and lots of onstage banter but without extended jam explorations.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar tone praised as “magic twanger”; vocal delivery powerful (occasionally overdriven on Bertha) and he leads many songs with confidence.
band_member_comments.Bob: Provides strong rhythm‑guitar work, energetic vocals (especially on Good Lovin’), and a few humorous moments that fans note.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboard presence noted as solid; occasional “someone banging those keys” comment indicates the organ adds texture throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Kreutzmann’s drumming singled out for being especially crisp and driving; Bill’s drum work on Good Lovin’ described as a “drum core.”
